title: Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter | Stats NZ
content:
## Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter
04 June 2026, 10:45am
Value of building work statistics estimate the value and volume of work put in place on construction jobs in New Zealand.
- Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter Excel spreadsheet, 95 KB

### Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter
Excel spreadsheet, 95 KB
- Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter – CSV ZIP compressed file, 389 KB

### Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter – CSV
ZIP compressed file, 389 KB
- Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter – updates to previously published statistics Excel spreadsheet, 29 KB

### Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter – updates to previously published statistics
Excel spreadsheet, 29 KB
In the March 2026 quarter:
- the seasonally adjusted total building volume fell 3.5 percent compared with the December 2025 quarter – residential fell 2.2 percent, and non-residential fell 4.0 percent
- total building value was $7.2 billion, down 5.9 percent from the March 2025 quarter.
Statistics remain provisional for the latest three quarters and are updated each quarter.
For a summary of the latest data updates, see ‘Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter – updates to previously published statistics’ under Download data .

## Seasonally adjusted building activity volume
The seasonally adjusted building activity volume series removes the effects of typical seasonal patterns and changes to construction costs. Volumes are calculated at September 2022 quarter prices.
Residential construction prices rose 0.5 percent in the March 2026 quarter, while non-residential prices rose 0.3 percent (as measured by the capital goods price index).
The total seasonally adjusted building volume fell 3.5 percent in the March 2026 quarter (following a 0.6 percent fall in the December 2025 quarter). Within this:
- residential building fell 2.2 percent (following a 1.1 percent rise in the December 2025 quarter)
- non-residential building fell 4.0 percent (following a 5.3 percent fall in the December 2025 quarter).
Percentage change in seasonally adjusted volume of building work put in place, by building type, March 2024–2026 quarters

## Regional actual value of building work
In value terms (not adjusted for cost changes or seasonal effects), the actual value of building work was $7.2 billion in the March 2026 quarter, down 5.9 percent from the March 2025 quarter.
Within this, residential building work was down 2.5 percent to $4.6 billion, and non-residential work was down 11 percent to $2.6 billion.
By region, the actual value of total building work in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter, was:
- $2.8 billion in Auckland (down 5.2 percent)
- $588 million in Waikato (down 9.8 percent)
- $541 million in Wellington (down 14 percent)
- $1.1 billion in the rest of the North Island (down 8.9 percent)
- $1.2 billion in Canterbury (down 3.8 percent)
- $894 million in the rest of the South Island (up 1.1 percent).
Value of building work put in place ($), by region, March 2011–2026 quarters
Rest of North Island
Rest of South Island

## Actual value of building work on non-residential buildings
The non-residential building types with the most work put in place (by value) in the year ended March 2026 were:
- offices, administration, and public transport buildings at $2.1 billion (down 5.2 percent compared with the year ended March 2025)
- hospitals, nursing homes, and health buildings at $1.7 billion (down 11 percent)
- education buildings at $1.6 billion (down 0.2 percent)
- factories and industrial buildings at $1.6 billion (down 8.9 percent).
Value of building work put in place for non-residential buildings ($), by building type, year ended March 2024–2026

## Statistics for the June 2025 quarter onwards updated
Statistics for the June 2025 quarter onwards were updated to reflect improved survey information. Data updates reflect:
- improved information from respondents in the full-coverage survey of high-value projects
- improved model adjustments (based on the survey of lower-value projects).
Methodology for value of building work put in place: June 2023 quarter has further detail.
Use Infoshare for time-series data about building work put in place:
Subject category: Industry sectors Survey name: Building activity survey – BAS Related survey: Building consents – BLD

## Definitions and metadata
Value of building work put in place – DataInfo+ provides the general methodology used from the December 2014 quarter estimates of the value of building work put in place, and related metadata.
Value of building work put in place concepts – DataInfo+ provides definitions of terms used from the December 2014 quarter.
